providerExtensions.name: Built-In MCP Marketplace for Server Discovery and Installation
providerExtensions.description: Cline includes a dedicated MCP Marketplace UI accessible from the MCP Servers icon in the top navigation bar. Users can browse and install MCP servers directly from within the tool without visiting external sites. This is in addition to community directories like mcpservers.org, mcp.so, and the official MCP GitHub repo.

providerExtensions.name: AI-Assisted MCP Server Setup via Conversation
providerExtensions.description: Users can add MCP servers by providing Cline with a GitHub repo URL (optionally with README contents). Cline clones the repo, builds it, and adds the configuration automatically, asking clarifying questions (e.g., about API keys, build commands) as needed. This conversational setup flow is unique to Cline and reduces manual JSON editing.

providerExtensions.name: alwaysAllow List for Auto-Approving Specific MCP Tools
providerExtensions.description: Each server entry in cline_mcp_settings.json supports an alwaysAllow array of tool names. Tools in this list are executed without user confirmation. This is server-scoped and tool-name-specific — it does not blanket-allow all tools on a server. Complements the global auto-approval feature.

providerExtensions.name: Global MCP Mode Setting for Token Usage Control
providerExtensions.description: A Cline>Mcp:Mode setting controls how MCP servers affect token usage globally. Accessible via the MCP Servers icon > Configure tab > Advanced MCP Settings. The exact mode options are not detailed in the documentation source, but the setting targets token efficiency for users with many MCP servers.

providerExtensions.name: SSE Transport Support for Remote MCP Servers
providerExtensions.description: In addition to STDIO (local child process), Cline supports SSE transport for remote MCP servers over HTTP/HTTPS. SSE server config uses a url field instead of command/args, plus optional headers (e.g., Authorization). This enables centrally-hosted MCP servers shared across multiple users without per-user installation.
